examples: Away but the hoose and tell me whae's there.

definitions.text: Merely, only, just, no more than
examples: Christmas comes but once a year, so we can but wait till then.
I'll have to go home early but.

definitions.text: Used to repeat an adverb for emphasis; simply; just; absolutely.
examples: I would never, but never do such a thing!

definitions.text: However, although, nevertheless, on the other hand .
examples: She is very old but still attractive.
She’s welcomed them but been rude to us.
I am not rich but [I am] poor. Not John but Peter went there.
It’s not so much that we don’t wanna go, but I just haven’t time.

definitions.text: Used at the beginning of a sentence to express opposition to a remark.
examples: But I never said you could do that!

definitions.text: An instance of using the word "but"; an objection or caveat.
examples: It has to be done—no ifs, ands, or buts.
But—and this is a big but—you have to come home by sundown.

definitions.text: The outer room of a small two-room cottage.
